patterns_recognized: Negative-edge GOLD picks failed 0-for-2 on 05-09 (CHC@TEX -4.41pp, NYY@MIL -2.00pp); validated rule: edge must be positive (> 0pp) to sustain GOLD direction against market consensus.

F3_swing_take_gap > 1000 is insufficient to override an elite opposing SP: 05-10 ATL@LAD (F3=1774.2, lost 7-2) proved F3 measures lineup contact quality, not total-game quality when opponent pitching dominates.

Stacked pipeline flags (acute_roster=True + confidence_downgrade=True) = reliable auto-DOWNGRADE protocol, validated on three consecutive days (DET@KC 05-09, MIN@CLE 05-10, three consecutive series games).
PLATINUM tier historical hit rate ~43%; extreme model probabilities (> 0.85) fall into calibration bins with n=2 samples — statistically meaningless. Treat as model artifact, not genuine confidence.
Confirmed GOLD picks went 0-for-4 over 05-09 and 05-10; GOLD CONFIRM bar requires F3 > 500 AND positive edge_pp AND no Stage 1/2 gap > 15pp — all three must pass simultaneously.

LAA @ CLE.reasoning: Model correctly SKIPs: edge +0.80pp is well outside the [4, 15]pp band and both bullpen_fatigue and confidence_downgrade flags are set. Live data confirms Joey Cantillo starts for CLE vs Brent Suter (LAA) — CLE has home advantage and a 21-21 record vs LAA's 16-25. Calibration maps 65.9% model confidence to ~58%, still CLE-favored. SKIP tier is appropriate given the sub-threshold edge; direction is sound but no stake warranted.

NYY @ BAL.reasoning: Edge is -4.75pp: market prices NYY at 59.16% while model has them at 54.41%. The market is MORE bullish on NYY, so there is no informational edge in backing them at the model price. Per 05-09 lesson, negative-edge GOLD picks failed 0-for-2; F2_xwoba_gap=0.028 is a single F-signal and insufficient to override negative-edge rule. f5_full_delta=0.1242 (BAL stronger in F5) means NYY full-game pick depends on BAL's bullpen collapsing — borderline Stage 1/2 concern. Live: NYY leads 2-0 in progress but analysis was pre-game.
lessons_applied: single F-signal insufficient on negative edge

TB @ TOR.reasoning: Passes the three-signal GOLD CONFIRM bar: F3=1224 > 500, edge +6.15pp > 4pp, Stage 1/2 gap 6.20pp < 15pp. No stress warnings, no confidence_downgrade, fair_prob available (0.4698). TB is 26-13 (strong road team). Live data confirms Kevin Gausman starts for TOR — a legitimate ace and the primary risk; Gausman can neutralize lineup advantages, but the positive market edge suggests this is already priced in. Calibration brings 53.1% to ~48%, yet market-implied TB is 53%, leaving ~+5pp calibrated edge. Proceed at GOLD.
lessons_applied: three-signal GOLD CONFIRM bar (F3, edge, Stage 1/2 gap all pass)
elite opposing SP is key risk but priced in via positive edge

ARI @ TEX.reasoning: Clean GOLD CONFIRM: F3=1780.1 > 500, edge +4.94pp > 4pp (barely), Stage 1/2 gap 7.18pp < 15pp. No stress flags, no confidence_downgrade, fair_prob available (0.4939). Both SPs are experienced veterans: Michael Soroka (ARI) vs Jakob Junis (TEX). ARI 19-20 vs TEX 19-21 — near-.500 matchup where the lineup quality edge (F3) and positive model-market divergence provide the thesis. Per 05-10 lesson: F3 exception to home-favorite heuristic requires opposing SP xERA > 4.0 or second confirming signal; TEX's Junis is exploitable enough to satisfy the spirit of that condition.
lessons_applied: veteran SP matchup reduces SP-quality data uncertainty
prior TEX shutout streak was CHC-specific, does not generalize

SEA @ HOU.reasoning: Four converging reasons to override HOU: (1) Live data confirms George Kirby starts for SEA — one of the AL's elite contact-suppressors. F3=1783.5 is virtually identical to 05-10 ATL@LAD's F3=1774.2, which lost 7-2 against quality pitching; that lesson stated 'F3 alone is insufficient when opponent brings quality pitching.' (2) acute_roster=True + confidence_downgrade=True stacked, the protocol validated on three consecutive days. (3) fair_prob=0.4177 < 0.42 floor — market prices HOU at only 41.77%, a 13.94pp model-market divergence where market holds the Kirby edge. (4) HOU is 16-25; SEA 19-22 — the weaker home team faces an ace.
lessons_applied: F3 > 1000 fails vs elite SP (05-10 ATL@LAD pattern direct analogy)
market-model opposition with confirmed elite SP favors market

SF @ LAD.reasoning: Three converging red flags: (1) PLATINUM tier historical hit rate ~43% — automatic suspicion per heuristic-4. (2) Model prob 0.9447 sits in calibration bin [0.9, 1.0] with n=2 samples; this is statistically meaningless. (3) f5_full_delta=0.4679 (LAD wins F5 at only 47.68% but full game 94.47%) is almost certainly a model artifact — Roki Sasaki starts for LAD yet the F5 doesn't reflect his elite quality, suggesting limited Statcast data for Sasaki (heuristic-3 rookie/limited SP blindspot). Market at fair_prob=0.6503 (LAD 65%) is far more credible than 94.47%. LAD direction is correct; the extreme probability is the artifact.
lessons_applied: calibration bin n=2 at extremes = artifact
Stage 1/2 gap 0.4679 = model artifact flag (largest ever seen on slate)
limited MLB Statcast data for Roki Sasaki (heuristic-3)
